chunks.page_summary: This page provides examples of Modbus TCP command and response structures for reading temperature and viscosity parameters from Rheonics devices.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Modbus TCP Command and Response Examples

Modified on: Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 3:30 PM

Reading the parameter 3; Temperature median, in float32 data type.
Address =56 = 0x0038 (word) (Address in Hex of the first register)
Functional code= 04=0x0004 (word)-Read input registers
Number of registers = 02=0x0002 (word)
Unit ID = 255 = 0xFF

Command Structure:
Trans. ID (Hi Byte) | Trans. ID (Lo Byte) | Protocol ID (Hi Byte) | Protocol ID (Lo Byte) | Length (Hi byte) | Length (Lo byte) | Unit ID | Function Code | Address 1st reg. (Hi Byte) | Address 1st reg. (Lo Byte) | # of registers (Hi Byte) | # of registers (Lo Byte)
00 | 00 | 00 | 00 | 00 | 06 | FF | 04 | 00 | 38 | 00 | 02

Command Response:
Functional Code | Number of Bytes | Value of the first register (Hi Byte) | Value of the first register (Lo Byte) | Value of the second register (Hi Byte) | Value of the second register (Lo Byte)
04 | 04 | 41 | B1 | 5C | 29

040441B15C29
Data = 0x41B15C29 = 22.17 (float32)

Reading the Parameter 1; Viscosity median, status
Address =43 = 0x002B (word) (Address in Hex of the first register)
Functional code= 04=0x0004 (word)-Read input registers
Number of registers = 01=0x0001 (word)
Unit ID = 255 = 0xFF

Command Structure:
key_facts: The document provides Modbus TCP command and response examples.
The document was last modified on Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 3:30 PM.
Parameter 3, 'Temperature median', is read using a float32 data type.
The Modbus address for Temperature median (Parameter 3) is 56 (0x0038).
Functional code 04 (0x0004) is used for 'Read input registers'.
Reading Temperature median requires 2 registers (0x0002 words).
The Unit ID used in the examples is 255 (0xFF).
An example Modbus TCP command for Temperature median has a length of 06 bytes.
The response for Temperature median includes the functional code 04, 04 bytes of data, and the values 41, B1, 5C, 29 for the two registers.
The hex data 0x41B15C29 corresponds to a float32 value of 22.17.
Parameter 1, 'Viscosity median, status', is read from Modbus address 43 (0x002B).
Reading Viscosity median requires 1 register (0x0001 word).

chunks.page_summary: This page details Modbus-like command structures and responses for reading an error status and the raw temperature (Int16) from a device, including its conversion to actual temperature.

content.title: Command Response
headers: Value of the first register (Hi Byte)
Value of the first register (Lo Byte)

content: Referring to the parameter status, this value means a general error occurred.

extracted_text.section_title: Reading the Parameter 8; Temperature raw, Int16 value.
content: Address =98 = 0x0062 (word) (Address in Hex of the first register)

content.title: Command Response
headers: Value of the first register (Hi Byte)
Value of the first register (Lo Byte)

content: Since the register value is the temperature scaled by 100. The actual temperature value is 22.06 °C.
This register is useful for devices in which single point conversion requires too much computation.

key_facts: A command response data of 0x0001 (1, Uint16) indicates a general error occurred.
Parameter 8 is identified as "Temperature raw, Int16 value".
The address for reading Parameter 8 (Temperature raw) is 0x0062 (decimal 98), which is the address in Hex of the first register.
The functional code used for reading input registers is 0x04 (0x0004 word).
The number of registers to read for temperature is 0x0001 (1 word).
The Unit ID for the device is 0xFF (decimal 255).
The raw temperature register value 0x089E (decimal 2206, Int16) represents the temperature scaled by 100.
The actual temperature value calculated from the raw register value is 22.06 °C.
This raw temperature register is useful for devices where single point conversion requires too much computation.
warnings_notes: Referring to the parameter status, a data value of 0x0001 means a general error occurred.
The raw temperature register is useful for devices in which single point conversion requires too much computation.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the Modbus command structure and response for reading multiple parameters, specifically the viscosity average value (float32 and UInt16) and its status.
chunks.extracted_text:
Reading Multiple parameters; Viscosity Average value in float, Uint and status.

Address =64 = 0x0040 (word) (Address in Hex of the first register)
Functional code= 04=0x0004 (word)-Read input registers
Number of registers = 04=0x0004 (word)
Unit ID = 255 = 0xFF

Command Structure:
| Trans. ID (Hi Byte) | Trans. ID (Lo Byte) | Protocol ID (Hi Byte) | Protocol ID (Lo Byte) | Length (Hi byte) | Length (Lo byte) | Unit ID | Function Code | Address 1st reg. (Hi byte) | Address 1st reg. (Lo Byte) | # of registers (Hi Byte) | # of registers (Lo Byte) |
| 00 | 00 | 00 | 00 | 00 | 06 | FF | 04 | 00 | 40 | 00 | 04 |

Command Response
| Functional Code | Number of Bytes | 1th reg. (Hi Byte) | 1th reg. (Lo Byte) | 2th reg. (Hi Byte) | 2th reg. (Lo Byte) | 3th reg. (Hi Byte) | 3th reg. (Lo Byte) | 4th reg. (Hi Byte) | 4th reg. (Lo Byte) |
| 04 | 08 | 41 | F4 | 51 | EC | 0B | EE | 00 | 00 |

040841F451EC0BEE0000
Viscosity Average (float32) = 30.54 cP
Viscosity Average (UInt16) = 3054 cP
Viscosity Average Status (UInt16) = 0
key_facts: The document provides details for reading multiple parameters, including viscosity average and its status, using a Modbus-like protocol.
The Modbus address for the first register is 0x0040 (decimal 64).
The functional code used for reading input registers is 0x0004 (decimal 4).
A total of 4 registers are requested in the command.
The Modbus Unit ID is 0xFF (decimal 255).
The command response includes 8 bytes of data for the registers.
An example command response value of 040841F451EC0BEE0000 is shown.
The example response translates to a float32 viscosity average of 30.54 cP.
The example response translates to an unsigned 16-bit integer viscosity average of 3054 cP.
The example response shows an unsigned 16-bit integer viscosity average status of 0.
